paper_title: Experimental Methods for Studying Foraminiferal Calcification under Anoxia

methods.method_name: Sediment Core Preparation and Aquarium Setup
methods.description: Procedure for preparing sediment cores and establishing the controlled aquarium environment for the experiment, including an acclimation period.

steps.description: For each experiment, five cores were filled with natural sediment, sampled at the same sites where each foraminiferal species was also collected (see the next paragraphs for details), and sieved (< 38 µm) without the addition of water by using a brush to press the sediment through the sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: Fill experimental cores with natural sediment and sieve sediment (< 38 µm) without water.

steps.description: The cores were then placed in an aquarium filled with approximately 20 L of bubbled artificial seawater (ASW) (Red Sea salt in MilliQ water) and kept under controlled conditions of salinity and temperature in the dark, to avoid algal blooms and the consequent geochemical instability.
steps.searchable_summary: Place filled cores in an aquarium with bubbled artificial seawater and maintain controlled salinity, temperature, and dark conditions.
inputs: Artificial seawater (ASW, Red Sea salt in MilliQ water)

steps.description: The cores were left 30 days in the aquarium before starting the experiment, in order to retrieve a relative stability of geochemical parameters of the cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Allow cores to acclimate in the aquarium for 30 days to achieve geochemical stability.

methods.description: Procedure for setting up specialized cores designed to prevent foraminiferal migration and introducing calcein-labelled foraminifera at the start of the experiment (T start).

steps.description: Foraminifera were introduced in the three remaining special cores (Fig. 1), constituted of superposed Plexiglas rings (4 cm ∅, 1-3 cm high), which were each separated by 100 µm mesh nylon nets to avoid foraminiferal migration.
steps.searchable_summary: Assemble special experimental cores using superposed Plexiglas rings separated by 100 µm mesh nylon nets.

steps.description: After one month (T start ), the foraminiferal replicate cores were opened under an N 2 -flushed atmosphere to introduce calcein-labelled foraminifera following Bernhard et al. (2004).
steps.searchable_summary: At T start (after 30 days), open foraminiferal cores under N2 atmosphere and introduce calcein-labelled foraminifera.
inputs: Assembled special cores (after 30 days in aquarium)

steps.description: The cores were then returned to the aquaria after being introduced in plastic bags filled with sediment, to avoid any further possible lateral oxygen penetration.
steps.searchable_summary: Return foraminifera cores to aquaria after placing them in sediment-filled plastic bags.
outputs: Foraminifera cores placed back in aquarium

methods.method_name: Foraminifera Processing and Vitality Assessment (T end)
methods.description: Procedure for processing foraminifera samples collected at the end of the experiment (T end) to assess new chamber formation (calcification) and vitality using fluorescent probes.

steps.description: At the end of the experiment, sixty days later (T end ), each sediment layer of the foraminiferal cores was sieved (100 µm) with ASW, and the foraminifera were picked.
steps.searchable_summary: At T end (sixty days later), sieve sediment layers from foraminiferal cores (100 µm) with ASW and pick foraminifera.

steps.description: A two-step observation was performed on each specimen in order to avoid any possible bias related to the use of two fluorescent probes (calcein and fluorescine diacetate) that excite and emit at similar wavelengths (Fig. 2). First, the presence of newly formed chambers, not calcein labelled, was checked using epifluorescence microscopy (Nikon SMZ 1500, 460-490 nm excitations) for the specimens of a core layer (a process which took approximately 1 h).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform first step of two-step observation: Check for newly formed, non-calcein-labelled chambers using epifluorescence microscopy (460-490 nm excitation).
inputs: Epifluorescence microscope (e.g., Nikon SMZ 1500, 460-490 nm excitation)
outputs: Identification of specimens with new chambers

steps.description: Only thereafter the foraminifera were incubated for 24 h in a 10 µM solution of fluorescein diacetate (FDA) in ASW (Bernhard et al., 1995) and assessed for vitality under epifluorescence microscopy. ... Moreover, specimens with new chambers were incubated separately from the others in an FDA solution, in order to avoid any mixing of samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate foraminifera specimens (separating those with new chambers) in a 10 µM FDA solution in ASW for 24 hours.
inputs: Fluorescein diacetate (FDA) solution (10 µM in ASW)

steps.description: The much more intense fluorescence of FDA-labelled cells compared to dead calcein-labelled specimens allowed us to distinguish dead and live foraminifera easily, even in the presence of calcein-labelled chambers (see Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: Assess vitality (live/dead) of FDA-labelled specimens using epifluorescence microscopy based on FDA fluorescence intensity.

methods.description: Procedure for sampling sediment cores designated for geochemical analysis at the start (T start) and end (T end) of the experiment to obtain pore water.

steps.description: At this time [T start], the first geochemical core was removed from the aquarium and sliced under an N 2 -flushed atmosphere to obtain pore waters for geochemical analyses (see below for details).
steps.searchable_summary: At T start, remove the first geochemical core from the aquarium and slice it under N2 atmosphere to obtain pore water.
inputs: Geochemical core (one of the five prepared)

steps.description: At the end of the experiment, the remaining geochemical core was sliced for pore water analyses at T end .
steps.searchable_summary: At T end, slice the remaining geochemical core to obtain pore water.
